jokes.text: You're entering a realm which is unusual. Maybe it's magic or contains some kind of monster. The second one.
jokes.setup: Parody of Twilight Zone's mysterious opening, building suspense
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflates the mystery with matter-of-fact clarification
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody structure - sets up Twilight Zone mystique then immediately undercuts it with casual directness

jokes.text: A casino where I'm winning? That car must have killed me. I must be in heaven.
jokes.setup: Character realizes he's in an impossible scenario
jokes.punchline: Logic leap that winning at casino = death and heaven
jokes.explanation: Standard Twilight Zone parody setup - functional but predictable

jokes.text: A casino where I always win? That's boring. I must really be... in hell!
jokes.setup: Realizes constant winning would be boring
jokes.punchline: Flips heaven assumption to hell
jokes.explanation: Predictable escalation but solid structure

jokes.text: No. You're not in heaven or hell. You're on an airplane.
jokes.setup: Built up metaphysical dilemma over two scenes
jokes.punchline: Mundane reality undercuts entire premise
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - completely deflates the philosophical setup with mundane reality

jokes.text: Why should I believe you? You're Hitler. Eva Braun, help me!
jokes.setup: Gremlin panic from Twilight Zone episode
jokes.punchline: Random historical villain appearance
jokes.explanation: Random absurdity works in context of parody but lacks deeper structure

jokes.text: Saw it coming.
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive reaction to obvious parody
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bender's cynical dismissal fits his personality

jokes.text: A big floating ball that lit up with every color in the rainbow... plus some new ones that were so beautiful I cried.
jokes.setup: Fry describing something amazing he saw
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic description of what's clearly a sign
jokes.explanation: Great character writing - Fry's naive wonder contrasted with mundane reality

jokes.text: They have a kid wear that to attract customers.
jokes.setup: Fry's poetic description builds up something transcendent
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just an advertising gimmick
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Fry's wonder with mundane reality

jokes.text: I don't care if it was a dork in a costume. For a moment, I felt the heartbeat of creation, and it was one with my own.
jokes.setup: Already revealed as just a costume
jokes.punchline: Fry doubles down on cosmic significance
jokes.explanation: Excellent character consistency - Fry refuses to let reality diminish his wonder

jokes.text: Big deal. We all feel like that all the time. You don't hear us gassing on about it.
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive response while clearly not feeling anything
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - claims universal experience while being emotionally vacant robot

jokes.text: Boring? Wasn't that the period when they cracked the human genome... and boy bands roamed the earth?
jokes.punchline: Pairs scientific achievement with cultural plague as equal accomplishments
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of genuine achievement with cultural embarrassment as historical markers

jokes.text: You know what might be a hoot? - No. Why would I know that?
jokes.punchline: Bender's literal interpretation and dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - takes rhetorical question literally and dismissively

jokes.text: Everything? - Except that.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to do everything Fry ever wanted
jokes.punchline: Immediate exclusion implies sexual fantasy
jokes.explanation: Economical sex joke - the exclusion tells the whole story

jokes.text: So your fantasy has always been to destroys a planet? - Yeah. What did they ever do for me?
jokes.setup: Reveal that Fry's fantasy is planetary destruction
jokes.punchline: Petty personal grievance justification
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - scales cosmic destruction down to personal slight

jokes.text: Funny, you live in the universe... but you never do these things till someone visits.
jokes.punchline: Universal truth about local attractions applied to universe itself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant scaling up of relatable tourism observation to cosmic level

jokes.text: Far out. So there's an infinite number of parallel universes? - No, just the two.
jokes.setup: Standard sci-fi concept of infinite parallel universes
jokes.punchline: Mundane limitation deflates the concept
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of infinite universe trope with arbitrary limitation

jokes.text: Well, I'm sure that's enough.
jokes.setup: Learning there are only two universes
jokes.punchline: Polite acceptance of cosmic disappointment
jokes.explanation: Nice character beat - Fry's polite disappointment at cosmic limitation

jokes.text: I'm sick of parallel Bender lording his cowboy hat over me.
jokes.punchline: Petty jealousy over minor fashion accessory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - reduces infinite possibility to petty fashion envy

jokes.text: Mommy, why is that man like that? - Don't look at him.
jokes.punchline: Public reaction to his behavior
jokes.explanation: Standard embarrassment humor - functional but predictable

jokes.text: The tongue tickles.
jokes.punchline: Childlike wonder at dangerous situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's naive enthusiasm

jokes.text: These new hands are great. I'll break them in tonight.
jokes.punchline: Sexual implication with robot body parts
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender innuendo with robot twist

jokes.text: I could pound your head till you think that's what happened.
jokes.setup: Offering to help with Fry's fantasy
jokes.punchline: Violence as solution to impossible desire
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly in character - Bender's violent 'helpfulness'

jokes.text: Of course. You should read a blimp.
jokes.punchline: Misuse of 'read' with blimp advertising
jokes.explanation: Good futuristic advertising joke with verbal confusion

jokes.text: Download a celebrity. What part of that do I understand?
jokes.punchline: Honest assessment of his confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - honest about his technological confusion

jokes.text: While you're there, pick me up a few credit card numbers.
jokes.punchline: Casual request for identity theft
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - treats cybercrime as casual favor

jokes.text: Kirk could kick Picard's ass. - At least Picard had the guts to admit he was bald.
jokes.setup: Classic internet argument about Star Trek captains
jokes.punchline: Unexpected angle on the debate
jokes.explanation: Good internet culture parody with unexpected character analysis

jokes.text: Sold to the being of inconceivable horror.
jokes.punchline: Casual sale to cosmic horror
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of eBay absurdity to cosmic level

jokes.text: Will a money order be okay? - Yes.
jokes.punchline: Mundane payment method concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect mundane bureaucracy meets cosmic horror

jokes.text: Gwyneth Paltrow? - Nah. I read she drinks human blood.
jokes.punchline: Casual vampire accusation about real celebrity
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity roast disguised as casual conversation

jokes.text: I'd prefer someone with shaved underarms.
jokes.punchline: Modern grooming standards over historical significance
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific shallow priority over historical importance

jokes.text: Only woman to be named People's Sexiest Woman of the Year twice: In 2003 and then again in 2063.
jokes.punchline: Implies she's still attractive 60 years later
jokes.explanation: Decent aging joke with future timeline absurdity

jokes.text: I'd like the 2003 model.
jokes.setup: Different years of Lucy Liu available
jokes.punchline: Treating person like product with model years
jokes.explanation: Decent objectification humor with car shopping parallel

jokes.text: You're one sexy man, Philip J. Fry.
jokes.setup: Robot programmed to be attracted to Fry
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top compliment to obvious loser
jokes.explanation: Basic wish fulfillment humor - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Well, duh! She's programmed to like you. - But this is Lucy Liu, the only good actress of the 21st century.
jokes.punchline: Fry's ridiculous overstatement about Lucy Liu
jokes.explanation: Good character delusion with absurd century-spanning claim

jokes.text: Would you like to register me? - I'll remind you later, you hot stud.
jokes.punchline: Combines mundane computer prompt with flirtation
jokes.explanation: Clever blend of annoying software trope with robot girlfriend

jokes.text: When I feel so stuffed I can't eat... I use the restroom, and then I can eat more. - You should write a book. People need to know about the 'can eat more.'
jokes.punchline: Fry's amazement at basic bodily function
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - treats obvious concept as revolutionary discovery

jokes.text: My personality is mathematically derived from my movies... proportionately weighted by box office receipts.
jokes.setup: Fry commenting on her personality accuracy
jokes.punchline: Reveals shallow algorithm based on commercial success
jokes.explanation: Brilliant commentary on how celebrity personalities are constructed and commodified

jokes.text: You're cute. - You're cute. - You. - You. - Oh, dear. She's stuck in an infinite loop and he's an idiot.
jokes.setup: Cute exchange between Fry and robot
jokes.punchline: Professor's diagnosis of programming error vs stupidity
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with great character-specific punchline from Professor

jokes.text: That's love for you.
jokes.punchline: Treats malfunction as normal romantic behavior
jokes.explanation: Good cynical observation equating love with dysfunction

jokes.text: Humans dating robots is sick. You people wonder why I'm still single? It's 'cause all the fine robot sisters are dating humans!
jokes.punchline: Reveals selfish motivation behind moral stance
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Bender's moral outrage is actually sexual frustration

jokes.text: This is Fry's decision. - And he made it wrong, so it's time for us to interfere.
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction and intervention justification
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Professor's paternalistic logic

jokes.text: Stay away from our women! You've got metal fever, boy!
jokes.punchline: Robot defending robots from humans with racist terminology
jokes.explanation: Great role reversal with Bender as protective of robot women

jokes.text: He never took middle school hygiene. He never saw the propaganda film. - It's just lucky I keep a copy in the VCR at all times.
jokes.punchline: Professor's oddly specific and constant preparation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - weirdly prepared for this exact situation

jokes.text: Neat-o! A Marilyn Monroe-Bot. - You're a dreamboat, Billy Everyteen.
jokes.punchline: Generic teen name and over-the-top compliment
jokes.explanation: Good propaganda film parody with generic characters

jokes.text: Your house is across the street. - That's an awfully long way to go for making out.
jokes.punchline: Extreme laziness makes short distance seem impossible
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of laziness to absurd degree

jokes.text: Where are all the football stars? And where are the biochemists?
jokes.setup: Society falling apart from robot dating
jokes.punchline: Pairs jocks and nerds as equally important to society
jokes.explanation: Nice equal treatment of stereotypically opposite groups

jokes.text: All civilization was an effort to impress the opposite sex. - And sometimes the same sex.
jokes.punchline: Casual inclusion of same-sex attraction
jokes.explanation: Good casual inclusivity in educational film parody

jokes.text: Have you guessed the name of Billy's planet? It was Earth!
jokes.punchline: Twist that it's our planet all along
jokes.explanation: Standard twist ending but effective for the parody

jokes.text: I knew I should have shown him Electro Gonorrhea: The Noisy Killer.
jokes.punchline: Alternative educational film with ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake educational film title combining medical horror with sound effects

jokes.text: That Fry's a sicko pervert, I tell you. Dating a robot, it's an atrosamacy!
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation of 'atrocity' with 'astronomy'
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific malapropism from Hermes

jokes.text: But Fry's our friend, Bender. - Will you stifle there, meat bag?
jokes.punchline: Bender's aggressive dismissal with dehumanizing term
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender response but well-timed for the situation

jokes.text: You stifle, Bender. - Finally you're standing up to him. - Although he is completely right.
jokes.punchline: Amy immediately undermines her own praise
jokes.explanation: Good quick reversal showing Amy's conflicted loyalty

jokes.text: Who's in charge? - If you're an investor, you can dump your money in the hole.
jokes.setup: Looking for authority at internet company
jokes.punchline: Honest assessment of dot-com investment
jokes.explanation: Great dot-com bubble satire with brutal honesty

jokes.text: The Internet is about the free exchange and sale of other people's ideas.
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admits to intellectual property theft
jokes.explanation: Brilliant accidentally honest description of internet business model

jokes.text: No. Stay out. There's a guy going for the Tetris world record in there.
jokes.punchline: Dismisses cry for help as gaming session
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection about the screaming person

jokes.text: Help me! They've been holding me prisoner for 800 years. - The real Lucy Liu! The one you can see a movie of.
jokes.punchline: Fry's awkward way of distinguishing real from digital
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment with clumsy but logical distinction

jokes.text: It hurts!
jokes.punchline: Physical pain from digital duplication
jokes.explanation: Basic dark humor about digital copying process

jokes.text: Don't make me call the maintenance guy. - It's a bluff. - We can't take that chance. Move!
jokes.punchline: Employees treat maintenance guy as serious threat
jokes.explanation: Good workplace humor about maintenance authority

jokes.text: Please pick me up before you go-go. - She asked first.
jokes.punchline: Bender's arbitrary fairness principle
jokes.explanation: Decent Bender moment with pop culture reference

jokes.text: We'd have gotten away with it if it weren't for those meddling adults.
jokes.punchline: Scooby-Doo reference with age twist
jokes.explanation: Standard reference with small twist for adult context

jokes.text: Quite a masculine piece of metal, aren't you? - Don't look at me like that. If you're thinking of crossing the line, you can forget it. Bender don't bend that way.
jokes.punchline: Bender's rejection using his name as sexual pun
jokes.explanation: Good Bender pun using his name for sexual orientation joke

jokes.text: Bender don't bend that way.
jokes.punchline: Bender's rejection using his name as sexual orientation pun
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific pun using Bender's name for sexual orientation. Perfect economy and highly quotable.

jokes.text: I am Lucy Liu. Give me your spines.
jokes.punchline: Demands spines in threatening manner
jokes.explanation: Basic robot menace with specific anatomical demand

jokes.text: Looks like you're retaining water.
jokes.punchline: Medical condition applied to robot combat
jokes.explanation: Decent pun connecting medical term to robot internals

jokes.text: That was incredible, Bender. You're like Jackie Chan before he got doughy.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about Jackie Chan's career
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity roast disguised as praise

jokes.text: Shucks, I wasn't afraid of that robot. I'm pretty brave once you get past my macho exterior...
jokes.punchline: Claims to be brave past his macho exterior (implying he's not actually macho)
jokes.explanation: Good Bender self-contradiction about his own toughness

jokes.text: Take this, and that, and one of these.
jokes.punchline: Repetitive fighting catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Basic repetitive catchphrase for action sequence

jokes.text: Now that's a wave of destruction that's easy on the eyes.
jokes.setup: Watching attractive robots destroy the city
jokes.punchline: Focuses on appearance during disaster
jokes.explanation: Good character priority showing Fry's focus on attractiveness over danger

jokes.text: Now that's a wave of destruction that's easy on the eyes.
jokes.setup: Army of beautiful Lucy Liu bots causing destruction
jokes.punchline: Appreciating the aesthetic appeal of attractive destroyers
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of destruction with aesthetic appreciation. Nice economy and structure.

jokes.text: This is the best movie I've ever seen. It has a vampire and an explosion.
jokes.punchline: Simple criteria for good movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment showing his simple tastes and priorities

jokes.text: It's amazing the way you notice two things.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic amazement at his minimal observation skills
jokes.explanation: Great Leela sarcasm perfectly calibrated to Fry's intellectual level

jokes.text: Read the jar, Evelyn Wood.
jokes.setup: Someone asking who's in the jar
jokes.punchline: Reference to speed reading instructor for obvious text
jokes.explanation: Good Professor reference showing his age and impatience

jokes.text: If you want to see the real vampire, look in the mirror. - I can't. I'm a vampire.
jokes.punchline: Logic problem with vampire mirrors
jokes.explanation: Standard vampire logic joke within movie-within-show

jokes.text: They're forming a human pyramid... Of robots.
jokes.punchline: Contradiction in terms - robot human pyramid
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction in terminology for visual gag

jokes.text: We weren't counting on them being as smart as they are sexy.
jokes.punchline: Implies being very sexy equals being very smart
jokes.explanation: Good backhanded compliment structure

jokes.text: Hooray! - And don't eat it.
jokes.punchline: Immediate prohibition knowing his habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing everyone knows Zoidberg's tendencies

jokes.text: You'll be safe in here, sweetheart. Get lost, Pavarotti.
jokes.punchline: Dismisses opera legend casually
jokes.explanation: Good Fry priority showing robot girlfriend over cultural icon

jokes.text: I love you more than the moon and the stars... and the... Poetic image number 37 not found.
jokes.punchline: Computer error message interrupting poetry
jokes.explanation: Excellent blend of romance and technology failure - perfect robot malfunction

jokes.text: You saved us. Are you all right? - Yes, my love. I'll be just... Massive corn clog in port seven.
jokes.punchline: Technical malfunction interrupts dying words
jokes.explanation: Great romantic moment undercut by mechanical reality

jokes.text: That and the largest gold nugget in the world.
jokes.setup: Saying her image is all she has
jokes.punchline: Reveals she actually has massive wealth too
jokes.explanation: Good undermining of her emotional appeal with material wealth

jokes.text: I'll always remember you, Fry. - Memory deleted.
jokes.punchline: Immediate deletion contradicts promise
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark irony - promises remembrance then immediately forgets

jokes.text: Yeah, at our wedding. - It's true. Bender and I are in love.
jokes.setup: Suggestion Fry could get to know real Lucy Liu
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's now with Bender
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Bender's earlier rejection, now contradicted

jokes.text: Yeah, at our wedding. - It's true. Bender and I are in love.
jokes.setup: Fry hoping to get to know real Lucy Liu
jokes.punchline: Surprise reveal that she's now with Bender
jokes.explanation: Great twist ending with perfect Bender hypocrisy callback. Good structure and character consistency.

jokes.text: But... But... - Don't be a prude, Fry.
jokes.punchline: Bender calls him prudish after whole anti-robot dating stance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character hypocrisy - Bender calling Fry prudish after his own moral crusade
